description:
Advances: specled.coverage_capture

MINIMAL EARLY-SHIP CANDIDATE — stops garbage production immediately. lib/specled_ex/coverage/formatter.ex: init is inert (one-line stderr notice, no-op formatter) unless armed via Application.get_env(:specled_ex, :spec_cover_run) set by the mix task — closes the accidental :ex_unit-env smuggle and makes bare test_helper.exs wiring (the docs/adoption.md:134/:298 vector voyd_config followed) harmless. DELETE the MFA->{file,0} fabrication clause (formatter.ex:128-134), the empty-snapshot placeholder (103-104), and the group_by_file(_) -> [] laundering catch-all (118); unknown shapes become counted decode_errors surfaced into envelope.degraded. lib/specled_ex/coverage.ex: remove default_snapshot_fn/snapshot_target :_ defaults; explicit opts validation. Disarmed-formatter tests.

## Orchestrator addenda (admission, epic @ 14a22d4)

Store's v2 API landed in specled_-155.1 (build_envelope/1, write_v2/2, read_v2/1, read_status/1; v1 untouched). If you need to persist run status for refusals, use Store.read_status's sidecar contract via write paths — do not edit store.ex.

KNOWN TENSION you must resolve honestly (the merge gate runs the FULL suite): mix spec.cover.test currently relies on the formatter activating when installed, and test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs asserts artifact behavior that the fabrication clauses currently produce. Disarming + deleting fabrication WILL change observable behavior of the armed path. You are authorized to: (a) add the minimal arming lines (Application.put_env(:specled_ex, :spec_cover_run, ...)) to lib/mix/tasks/spec.cover.test.ex so the supported path stays functional — nothing else in that file (T3 owns its rework); (b) update existing formatter/task tests whose assertions the deleted fabrication clauses directly invalidate — the new honest behavior is the contract, garbage records must not survive. Keep the suite green; do not weaken tests that still describe intended behavior.

Spec co-change is authorized and required when the gate demands it: co-edit only the .spec/specs sections your diff directly invalidates. .spec/realization_hashes.json is tool-managed. A green gate outranks Out of Scope.

LESSON (from specled_-155.1): when touching files with api_boundary-bound functions, append new code below existing bound functions — line shifts above them trigger spurious realization drift (specled_-wp7).

## Allowed touches

- lib/specled_ex/coverage/formatter.ex
- lib/specled_ex/coverage.ex
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.cover.test.ex (ONLY the arming seam lines per addendum)
- test/specled_ex/coverage/formatter_test.exs
- test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs (only assertions invalidated by deleted fabrication)
- .spec/specs/coverage_capture.spec.md (only sections your diff invalidates + stub flips)
- .spec/realization_hashes.json (tool-regenerated)

## Out of Scope (files)

- lib/specled_ex/coverage/store.ex
- lib/specled_ex/coverage/aggregate.ex, lib/mix/tasks/spec.cover.ingest.ex (T2, in flight — avoid collisions)
- lib/specled_ex/coverage_triangulation.ex, lib/specled_ex/review/**
- docs/**

## Out of Scope (intent)

- No new external deps
- No per-test lane rebuild (native deltas/diffing is T5) — this ticket only disarms, arms, and stops fabrication
- Never :cover.reset

## Verification

- Bare wiring no-op: formatter registered via ExUnit.start(formatters: [...]) WITHOUT the arming env produces NO artifact and exactly one stderr notice (test via capture or child-BEAM fixture)
- Armed path: with :specled_ex :spec_cover_run set (as spec.cover.test now does), formatter activates as before
- Fabrication gone: an MFA-shaped (function-level) snapshot entry produces NO {file, 0} record; unknown/degenerate snapshot shapes are counted (decode_errors) and surfaced, not laundered into well-formed records
- Never-executed function is ABSENT from records
- Accidental :ex_unit env smuggle closed: formatter opts come only from the :specled_ex arming seam
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors, formatter + task tests, mix spec.check all green

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: impl-1
Log: /Users/mzubieta/.local/state/agentic-runs/specled_ex/specled_-155.4/codex-impl-1/log

Commit: 0970b64f0087e062980777c9a2c4a1e6f9d513f8
Files touched:
  lib/specled_ex/coverage/formatter.ex
  lib/specled_ex/coverage.ex
  lib/mix/tasks/spec.cover.test.ex (arming line only)
  test/specled_ex/coverage/formatter_test.exs
  .spec/specs/coverage_capture.spec.md
  .spec/decisions/specled.decision.serialized_per_test_coverage.md (amended, not in Allowed touches — see Deferred/notes below)
  .spec/realization_hashes.json (tool-regenerated: reseeded SpecLedEx.Coverage, SpecLedEx.Coverage.Formatter, SpecLedEx.Coverage.init/2, SpecLedEx.Coverage.install/1)

Tests added (SpecLedEx.Coverage.FormatterTest):
  "raises without :snapshot_fn"
  "raises without :snapshot_target"
  "without arming, init returns :disabled and prints exactly one stderr notice"
  "every event no-ops in the :disabled state, never crashing or writing"
  "arming with `true` activates production defaults (activates as before)"
  "arming with a keyword list injects explicit config (test seam)"
  "init/1's own argument is ignored — formatter opts come only from the :specled_ex seam"
  "real line-level coverage round-trips to a record, with no stderr noise"
  "genuinely empty coverage ({:result, [], []}) yields zero records, no placeholder"
  "an MFA-shaped (function-level) entry never produces a {file, 0} record"
  "a never-executed function (call count 0) is absent from records, not fabricated"
  "an unrecognized whole-snapshot shape is counted as a decode error and surfaced, not laundered"
  (plus existing init/install/test_finished tests updated to supply required explicit opts / arm via the seam)

Verification stubs flipped: none (new coverage_capture verification block for
formatter_arming_seam / formatter_no_fabrication authored directly as execute: true)

Verification tier: change-scoped — mix compile --warnings-as-errors; mix test
test/specled_ex/coverage/ test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs --include
integration (37 tests, 0 failures); mix format --check-formatted; mix
deps.unlock --check-unused; full mix spec.check (exit 0, only :info findings
remain). Full-system proof deferred to the merge gate / PR CI.
Final-tier runs: 1

Deferred must requirements: none omitted — all six Verification bullets
implemented and tested (bare wiring no-op, armed path, fabrication gone,
never-executed-function absence, env-smuggle closed, green
compile/tests/spec.check).

Summary: SpecLedEx.Coverage.Formatter is now disarmed by default (armed only
via Application.get_env(:specled_ex, :spec_cover_run), set solely by `mix
spec.cover.test`); init/1's own argument is never trusted since ExUnit
forwards its whole :ex_unit app env to every formatter. Deleted the
MFA->{file,0} fabrication clause, the empty-snapshot placeholder, and the
group_by_file(_)->[] laundering catch-all; unrecognized/function-level
snapshot shapes now count and surface as decode errors via stderr, never as
fabricated records. Root-caused and fixed a second bug in the same path:
the production snapshot_fn called bare :cover.analyse/1, which defaults to
function-level (not line-level) granularity — this is why the MFA
fabrication clause was the primary production code path, not a rare edge
case. Production snapshot_fn now explicitly requests
:cover.analyse(target, :coverage, :line). Coverage.init/2 no longer
silently defaults :snapshot_fn/:snapshot_target; it raises if either is
omitted.

Notes / conflicts surfaced per addendum ("green gate outranks Out of Scope"):
- This diff's realization drift for SpecLedEx.Coverage, .Formatter, .init/2,
  and .install/1 could only be cleared by deleting their stale entries in
  .spec/realization_hashes.json and letting `mix spec.check`'s silent-seed
  pass recompute them (Orchestrator.refresh_and_commit_hashes/3 only runs
  when no drift fired, so drift can't self-clear otherwise — same mechanism
  documented in this repo's own commit c9d769b). .install/1's drift was a
  pure line-shift artifact (specled_-wp7 strip_meta bug), no content change.
- The cross-cutting change (spans coverage_capture, branch_guard, mix_tasks,
  status subjects) tripped branch_guard_missing_decision_update. Rather than
  use a Spec-Drift escape hatch, I amended
  .spec/decisions/specled.decision.serialized_per_test_coverage.md (not
  listed in this ticket's Allowed touches) because the change is genuinely
  durable cross-cutting policy (disarm-by-default, no-silent-defaults,
  no-fabrication) worth recording, not a refactor to wave through.
- The remaining branch_guard_missing_subject_update finding for
  lib/mix/tasks/spec.cover.test.ex against specled.mix_tasks was resolved
  via a `Spec-Drift: branch_guard_missing_subject_update=info` commit
  trailer with a one-line reason — the one-line arming addition is fully
  described by coverage_capture.spec.md's new requirement and touches
  nothing specled.mix_tasks itself binds.
- lib/specled_ex/coverage.ex is also bound (whole-module) under
  branch_guard.spec.md and specled.status; both surfaced as :info-only
  findings (attested clean) since none of the functions those subjects
  care about (subject_file_map/covered_files/category_summary/subject_id)
  were touched — no action needed there.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: verify-1
Log: /Users/mzubieta/.local/state/agentic-runs/specled_ex/specled_-155.4/codex-verify-1/log

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 7
- Files outside Allowed globs: 1
  - .spec/decisions/specled.decision.serialized_per_test_coverage.md — GATE-FORCED. Cross-cutting change trips branch_guard_missing_decision_update; `mix spec.next --base origin/main` reports "The ADR update is already in the change set. Review it alongside the subject updates." Addendum's "green gate outranks Out of Scope" authorizes gate-demanded co-change; its literal grant names .spec/specs, but amending the exact ADR governing this formatter (rather than a Spec-Drift suppression trailer) is the honest resolution. Append-only clean: frontmatter byte-identical (affects / change_type:clarifies / reverses_what unchanged), status=accepted, no deletion; adr_append_only detector does not fire.
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 1 (new coverage_capture tagged_tests block, execute:true, covering formatter_arming_seam + formatter_no_fabrication), passed: 1. No pre-existing stubs were flipped false->true.
- Requirements coverage:
  - formatter_arming_seam: covered by formatter_test.exs "without arming, init returns :disabled and prints exactly one stderr notice" (assert {:ok,:disabled} + exactly 1 stderr line) and "init/1's own argument is ignored" (refute snapshot_fn==smuggled, assert snapshot_target==:_). Would fail if init trusted its own arg or failed to disarm. Seams: capture_io(:stderr), Application.put_env (the real arming seam, not a mock), snapshot_fn stub (legit DI seam).
  - formatter_no_fabrication: covered by "an MFA-shaped entry never produces a {file,0} record", "never-executed function absent", "unrecognized whole-snapshot shape counted". Would fail if any deleted fabrication clause returned (records would be non-empty; decode_errors=0). Seams: crafted stub snapshot_fn (legit DI seam).
  - formatter_snapshot_fn_di: covered by "real line-level coverage round-trips to a record" (lines_hit==[line]) and "arming with `true` activates production defaults" (is_function(snapshot_fn,1)). Would fail if formatter bypassed the injected fn. Seams: stub snapshot_fn. NOTE: the statement's claim that the production default calls :cover.analyse(target,:coverage,:line) is verified by source inspection (default_snapshot_fn/1), not by execution — the production fn is never run in unit tests (would require cover-compiled modules). Verdict-neutral.
- Governance checks (ADR append-only, Spec-Drift trailer): clean. ADR amendment mutates no immutable structural field and no status; adr_append_only cross_field detector silent (errors=0). Spec-Drift trailer `branch_guard_missing_subject_update=info` is a valid <code>=<severity> form per specled.spec_drift_trailer.parse_vocabulary with a one-line justification — sanctioned self-report, not a violation.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. No new deps (mix.exs untouched), no per-test lane rebuild / native deltas (diff only disarms, arms, stops fabrication), no :cover.reset anywhere in the diff.
- Targeted verification: pass. Flipped stub: coverage_capture tagged_tests (arming_seam + no_fabrication). Commands: mix compile --warnings-as-errors (clean); mix test test/specled_ex/coverage/formatter_test.exs (18/0); mix test test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs --include integration (1/0, armed path end-to-end); mix format --check-formatted (exit 0); mix deps.unlock --check-unused (clean); mix spec.check (status=pass, errors=0, warnings=0, exit 0). spec_cover_test_test.exs was NOT edited yet its armed-path assertion still passes — the arming line kept the supported path functional.
- Corpus-scoped claims: validated all testable identifiers in coverage_capture.spec.md and the ADR amendment whole-file (env keys :specled_ex/:spec_cover_run, {:ok,:disabled}, put_env in spec.cover.test.ex, config keys snapshot_fn/snapshot_target/modules_fn/artifact_path, {:result, ok_results, failed} shape, :cover.analyse(target,:coverage,:line), decode-error stderr notice at suite_finished). All match implementation; disarm/no-fabrication/arming claims validated by execution via the passing tagged tests. Falsehoods: none.
- Full-suite verification: not run by verifier (by design)
- Audit-expanded scope: none

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- The out-of-Allowed ADR edit is verdict-neutral: it was demanded by the branch-guard cross-cutting decision gate and is append-only clean. Reported for the orchestrator's ledger, not as a gap.
- formatter_snapshot_fn_di's production-default granularity (:line) is proven by source, not execution; acceptable since exercising the real :cover.analyse path needs cover-compiled modules outside unit scope.
- spec.next counts changed_files=8 vs 7 here because it diffs origin/main; mechanical scope uses the ticket base SHA 14a22d4 (7 files), which is authoritative for this ticket.

title: T4: Formatter disarm-by-default + arming seam + delete fabrication clauses
